differentials.raw: ataxia, cognitive dysfunction ← neurodegeneration (with T2-hyperintense lesions in cerebellum + basal ganglia)

differentials.raw: diabetes insipidus (most common) ← decreased secretion of antidiuretic hormone ← infiltration of posterior pituitary gland

differentials.raw: eosinophilia in blood + CSF Location:   limited to single / few bones (in children); may involve lung (in adults)

differentials.raw: ptosis, palpebral and periocular erythema, enlargement of associated palpebral fissure

differentials.raw: intractable otitis media with chronically draining ear (in temporal bone involvement)

differentials.raw: erythematous papules + plaques → become eroded + develop serous / hemorrhagic crust

differentials.name: Diabetes insipidus
differentials.raw: Diabetes insipidus (basilar skull disease / direct infiltration of posterior pituitary gland)

context: Christian syndrome ◊  Names should be disregarded as they were thought of formerly as different diseases! @   Bone (80%) √  bone lesions: in 80% (most common radiographic manifestation) Location:  predilection for flat bones (skull > mandible > rib > pelvis > spine) especially in adults ›  Skull: √  well-defined lytic "punched-out" lesion of skull √  characteristic beveled edge = asymmetric destruction of inner + outer cortices √  geographic skull = skull lesions may grow in size and coalesce N.B.:   calvarial disease lacks periosteal reaction DDx for single lesion:   epidermoid / dermoid cyst
